betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Crystallizer§f:
The Crystallizer (nuclearcraft:crystallizer) is a single-block machine that converts saturated solutions into dry crystalline dusts. It is the inverse of the Melter for fluid chemistry outputs.

§6Slot Layout (CrystallizerRecipes.java:11)§f:
0 item input → 1 fluid input → 1 item output → 0 fluid output. Each cycle precipitates one stack of dust from one fluid stack.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:109)§f:
Pattern "PSP / SCS / PUP" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: P = plateAdvanced, S = solenoidCopper, C = chassis, U = Items.CAULDRON (vanilla cauldron). Yields 1 Crystallizer.

§6What It Makes (CrystallizerRecipes.java:20-37)§f:
The Crystallizer recovers the following dusts from their saturated solutions (most at 1 GEM_VOLUME per recipe, except where noted):
- boron_nitride_solution → dustBoronNitride (1.0× speed, 1.0× power)
- fluorite_water → dustFluorite
- calcium_sulfate_solution → dustCalciumSulfate
- sodium_fluoride_solution → dustSodiumFluoride
- potassium_fluoride_solution → dustPotassiumFluoride
- sodium_hydroxide_solution → dustSodiumHydroxide (0.5×, 0.5×)
- potassium_hydroxide_solution → dustPotassiumHydroxide (0.5×, 0.5×)
- borax_solution → dustBorax (0.5×, 0.5×)
- irradiated_borax_solution → dustBorax (0.5×, 0.5×, applies Caesium-137 radiation at RadSources.CAESIUM_137/4 — this is the radioactive byproduct route)
- ammonium_sulfate_solution → dustAmmoniumSulfate
- ammonium_bisulfate_solution → dustAmmoniumBisulfate
- ammonium_persulfate_solution → dustAmmoniumPersulfate
- hydroquinone_solution → dustHydroquinone
- sodium_hydroquinone_solution → dustSodiumHydroquinone
- potassium_hydroquinone_solution → dustPotassiumHydroquinone

§6Silicon Carbide Fiber (CrystallizerRecipes.java:36)§f:
One special recipe: sic_vapor (INGOT_VOLUME) → fiberSiliconCarbide ×2 at 2.0× speed and 2.0× power. SiC fibers are the structural reinforcement used in advanced composite recipes.

§6Upstream Chain (CrystallizerRecipes.java:20-35)§f:
All of the Crystallizer's inputs are produced by the Chemical Reactor and the Fluid Mixer:
- boron_nitride_solution ← Chemical Reactor (boric_acid + ammonia)
- fluorite_water ← ingotFluorite via Melter
- calcium_sulfate_solution ← Chemical Reactor (fluorite_water + sulfuric_acid)
- sodium/potassium_fluoride_solution ← Chemical Reactor
- borax_solution ← Chemical Reactor
- hydroquinone_solution ← Chemical Reactor (phenol + hydrogen_peroxide)
- sodium/potassium_hydroquinone_solution ← Chemical Reactor

§6Irradiated Byproduct (CrystallizerRecipes.java:28)§f:
The irradiated_borax_solution → dustBorax recipe is the only one in NC that adds a radiation source (Caesium-137 at 1/4 intensity) to the output. This is a downstream effect of passing borax_solution through an active Fission Irradiator before re-crystallizing.

§eTip§f: The Crystallizer pairs perfectly with the Chemical Reactor — every saturated solution the reactor emits needs a Crystallizer to recover the dry dust form. Build them adjacent with fluid pipes for an automated chemistry line.

§eTip§f: Boron Nitride dust is the key input for advanced QMD components (BSCCO precursors, plasma containment). Setting up a stable boron_nitride_solution → dustBoronNitride loop is essential for QMD progression.

§cWarning§f: When crystallizing irradiated_borax_solution, the dust output will be radioactive. Use a Rad-Shielded Container or store it in a shielded room.
